question: If you ___ the button, the machine starts.
explanation: Zero conditional (fato/funcionamento): **Simple Present nos dois lados**. "If" aqui equivale a "when".

question: If she studies hard, she ___ the exam next week.
explanation: 1ª condicional (futuro possível, "next week"): If + Simple Present na condição e **will + verbo base** no resultado.

question: If I ___ you, I would apologize to her.
explanation: 2ª condicional com o verbo "to be": use **were** para todas as pessoas — "If I **were** you…".

question: We ___ the bus if we don't leave now.
explanation: 1ª condicional: o **will** fica na cláusula principal ("we **will miss**"), e o Simple Present fica no "if" ("if we don't leave").

question: If you mix blue and yellow, you ___ green.
explanation: Zero conditional (verdade geral/fato científico): **Simple Present** nas duas cláusulas — "you **get** green".

question: If it ___ cold tonight, I will close the windows.
explanation: 1ª condicional: dentro do "if" usamos **Simple Present** ("gets"), nunca **will** — esse é um erro clássico.

question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: If I will see him, I will tell him.
If I see him, I will tell him.
If I saw him, I will tell him.
If I see him, I would tell him.
explanation: 1ª condicional: **If + Simple Present** + will. Não se usa "will" no if, nem se mistura Simple Past com will.

question: If I had a car, I ___ to work every day.
explanation: 2ª condicional (hipótese no presente): If + Simple Past + **would + verbo base** = "eu **dirigiria**".

question: If they ___ the invitation, they would have come to the party.
explanation: 3ª condicional (passado irreal): **If + Past Perfect** (had received) combina com "would have come".

question: She ___ happier if she lived closer to her family.
explanation: 2ª condicional: "if she **lived**" (Simple Past) pede **would be** na cláusula principal — situação hipotética no presente.

question: If we had left earlier, we ___ stuck in traffic for two hours yesterday.
explanation: 3ª condicional (passado irreal, "yesterday"): **would have + particípio** — "wouldn't **have been** stuck".

question: Which sentence contains a mistake?
options: If you heat metal, it expands.
If she calls me, I will pick up.
If he had known, he would have said something.
If I would have money, I would lend it to you.
explanation: **Would nunca entra na cláusula do if**. O correto é "If I **had** money, I would lend it to you" (2ª condicional).

question: If dogs ___ talk, what would they say?
explanation: Hipótese irreal (2ª condicional): o passado de "can" é **could** — "If dogs **could** talk…" combina com "would say".

question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: I will call you, if I go.
If I go I will call you.
I will call you if I go.
If I will go, I will call you.
explanation: Quando o "if" vem **depois** da cláusula principal, **não usamos vírgula**. Se viesse antes, precisaria de vírgula: "If I go, I will call you."

question: If you had told me about the meeting, I ___ prepared a presentation.
explanation: 3ª condicional: If + Past Perfect + **would have + particípio**. Sem o "have", a frase fica incompleta.

question: If he ___ so busy, he would join us for lunch.
explanation: 2ª condicional negativa com "to be": use **weren't** para todas as pessoas — "If he **weren't** so busy…".

question: Transform: "I didn't study, so I failed." → "If I ___, I wouldn't have failed."
explanation: Fato real no passado vira 3ª condicional: **If + Past Perfect** ("had studied") + would have + particípio.

question: If the traffic ___ bad, we'll take the subway.
explanation: 1ª condicional (situação real e possível): **If + Simple Present** ("is") + will na cláusula principal.

question: You missed the flight because you woke up late. Complete: "If you ___ up earlier, you ___ the flight."
explanation: O fato já aconteceu no **passado** (você perdeu o voo) → 3ª condicional completa: **had + particípio** no if e **would have + particípio** no resultado.

question: Which sentence expresses an unreal situation in the PAST?
options: If I have time, I will help you.
If I had time, I would help you.
If I had had time, I would have helped you.
If I have had time, I would help you.
explanation: Passado irreal = **3ª condicional**: If + Past Perfect ("had had") + would have + particípio. A 2ª ("If I had time…") fala do **presente** hipotético.

question: She turned down the job offer last year. If she ___ it, she would be living in London now.
explanation: Condicional mista: condição **irreal no passado** ("last year") → **Past Perfect** (had accepted), mesmo com resultado no presente ("now").

question: Choose the correct sentence:
options: If I'd known, I would of helped.
If I'd known, I would've helped.
If I would know, I would've helped.
If I'd have known, I would helped.
explanation: **Would've = would have** (forma reduzida correta). "Would **of**" é um erro de escrita, e "would" não entra na cláusula do if.

question: You are very unlikely to win the lottery. Which sentence fits this idea?
options: If I win the lottery, I will buy a boat.
If I won the lottery, I will buy a boat.
If I won the lottery, I would buy a boat.
If I had won the lottery, I would have bought a boat.
explanation: Situação **improvável** no presente/futuro → **2ª condicional**: If + Simple Past + would. A 1ª seria para algo provável, e a 3ª para o passado.
